user_prompt: move as much of the new decrypted download functions inside `lib\WifiManager\DecryptedDownload.cpp` and `lib\WifiManager\DecryptedDownload.h`, protected by `BUILD_WITH_DECRYPTED_DOWNLOAD`
let the front-end 404, but it should be fine, I don't want to add a "allow_decrypted_download" into the json yet
agent_reply: Done. I moved the decrypted download implementation into:

`WebFileHandlers.cpp/.h` are back to raw microSD file handling only. `WebServer.cpp` now includes the new header and registers `/decrypted_download` only under `BUILD_WITH_DECRYPTED_DOWNLOAD`, calling `DecryptedDownload::finish` / `DecryptedDownload::writeBody` at [WebServer.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebServer.cpp:909).
I left the frontend alone, so if `BUILD_WITH_DECRYPTED_DOWNLOAD` is not enabled, the unlock request will just hit a missing route / 404 as requested. No `allow_decrypted_download` JSON flag added.

- Full `pio run -e thefly_sec1` is still blocked by unrelated dirty [main.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/main.cpp:145): `Aegis::setTestTempPassword("123456")` needs `const uint8_t*`, not `const char*`.

user_prompt: problem is not fixed, same symptom, second load of the file list returns blank
also, decrypted file download seems to return one chunk and then dies, the browser reports an error saying something about it being partial, 65.58kb if it helps
agent_reply: I made the next fix pass and rebuilt `thefly_sec1` successfully.

For `/list_files.json`, I kept the chunked response. The change is that a new file-list request now forcibly closes any previous active file-list stream before opening the SD root again: [WebFileHandlers.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp:528). That should address the “first load works, second load blank” case if the stale chunked response is keeping the directory handle/state alive.

For decrypted download, I did not paper over it by changing response mode. I added serial diagnostics at the exact places where the stream can die early: seek failure, short SD read, GCM auth failure, audio chunk failure, and final early-stop report: [DecryptedDownload.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/DecryptedDownload.cpp:617).
If the decrypted download still stops around 65.58 KB, the new serial log should now say whether it is a short SD read, bad chunk/auth, or a seek/math issue.

user_prompt: Make a `lib\WifiManager\AsyncFsManager.cpp` and its associated header file
- init, called from any activation of Wi-Fi
- reset walk, reset iterator for file listing, should always work no matter what state
- walk one, return one file from directory, the next call should return the next file, return something to indicate end of list when needed, automatically restart the walk if something is wrong
- open file for download, closes any other file or anything else that might conflict, reset seek pointer
critically, all of these need to be thread safe
web server code can only use methods within this manager, add thread safe functions to this manager as needed
use it to attempt to fix the problem with listing files and the decrypted download
agent_reply: Implemented `AsyncFsManager` and routed the web SD access through it.
- It owns the active walk handle and active download handle behind a recursive FreeRTOS mutex.
- Opening a download closes any active walk; resetting a walk closes any active download.
- Added thread-safe helpers for web upload/delete/readiness: `writeFileChunk`, `removeFile`, `isFile`, `isReady`.
- `/list_files.json` to use `AsyncFsManager::resetWalk()` and `walkOne()` instead of holding its own `FsFile`: [WebFileHandlers.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp:221)
- decrypted download to use the manager for encrypted recording reads: [DecryptedDownload.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/DecryptedDownload.cpp:505)

- `/list_files.json` to use `AsyncFsManager::resetWalk()` and `walkOne()` instead of holding its own `FsFile`: [WebFileHandlers.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebFileHandlers.cpp:221)
- decrypted download to use the manager for encrypted recording reads: [DecryptedDownload.cpp](/d:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/DecryptedDownload.cpp:505)

user_prompt: implement this, both front-end and back-end
agent_reply: Implemented the GET-based decrypted download flow.
- Frontend now encrypts the filename/action payload, base64url-encodes it, and navigates to:
- Browser should now treat the decrypted WAV as a normal download immediately, using the server’s `Content-Disposition`.
- Backend added `GET /decrypted_download` alongside the old POST path.
- Backend decodes the base64url request, authenticates using query params as fallback for the usual session headers, decrypts the request, then streams the WAV.
Implemented the GET-based decrypted download flow.
- Frontend now encrypts the filename/action payload, base64url-encodes it, and navigates to:
- Browser should now treat the decrypted WAV as a normal download immediately, using the server’s `Content-Disposition`.
- Backend added `GET /decrypted_download` alongside the old POST path.
- Backend decodes the base64url request, authenticates using query params as fallback for the usual session headers, decrypts the request, then streams the WAV.

user_prompt: no, only encrypt the file name with the session key, no other parameters, I see no reason why this wouldn't work in the backend if the backend has a full copy of the session key as well
agent_reply: Done. The decrypted download URL now carries only the encrypted filename:
No session response, no session salt, no JSON/action wrapper.
- Added `WebServer::copyCachedSessionKey()` so the GET handler can use the cached session key.
- `GET /decrypted_download` now decrypts only the `file_name` parameter with that cached session key.
- It validates the decrypted name as a `.rec` path, then streams it as an attachment.
- Encrypts just the raw filename string with the session AES key.
- Navigates to the GET URL so the browser treats it like a real download.
